name: The Inner Work
subtitle: A game of balance and lifelong growth

objectives.splits: Choose which capacities you lead with. Your opponent chooses simultaneously.
objectives.placing: Arrange your capacities. Your opening is your first act of self-awareness.
objectives.free_move: Player 2 adjusts before the work begins.
objectives.play_normal: Advance your capacities in partnership toward the center. No one grows alone.
objectives.play_holding: You hold the center. Three moves — keep your capacities advancing behind you.
objectives.play_berserker: Your patterns are broken. Move with urgency to restore balance.
objectives.wait_normal: Your opponent does their work. Observe. Prepare your response.
objectives.wait_holding: You hold the center — your opponent struggles to restore balance.
objectives.wait_berserker: Your opponent acts from crisis. Stay centered.
objectives.opponent_left: Your opponent has stepped away from the work. Growth pauses.
objectives.opponent_timeout: Your opponent has gone quiet. The inner work waits for no one.
objectives.finished: The work continues. But the shape of growth has been decided.

splits: The privilege of a lifetime is to become who you truly are. — Carl Jung
An unexamined life is not worth living. — Socrates
Knowing yourself is the beginning of all wisdom. — Aristotle
placing: Your opening arrangement is a self-portrait. How you place your capacities tells you which ones you trust most and which ones you keep at a distance.
Between stimulus and response there is a space. In that space is our power to choose our response. In our response lies our growth and our freedom. — Viktor Frankl
play: No man is an island, entire of itself. Every man is a piece of the continent, a part of the main. — John Donne
You do not have to be good. You do not have to walk on your knees for a hundred miles through the desert repenting. You only have to let the soft animal of your body love what it loves. — Mary Oliver
Growth is not meant to be done alone. The capacities we neglect most are usually the ones we most need a partner to model for us. — Brene Brown
We are not human beings having a spiritual experience. We are spiritual beings having a human experience. — Pierre Teilhard de Chardin
capture: You may encounter many defeats, but you must not be defeated. It may be necessary to encounter the defeats so you can know who you are, what you can rise from, how you can still come out of it. — Maya Angelou
Fall seven times, stand up eight. — Japanese proverb
The wound is the place where the light enters you. — Rumi
Displacement is not failure. When a capacity is pushed back it returns. It is humbled and reoriented, not destroyed.
center: The most terrifying thing is to accept oneself completely. — Carl Jung
Owning our story and loving ourselves through that process is the bravest thing we will ever do. — Brene Brown
To know what you prefer instead of humbly saying Amen to what the world tells you you ought to prefer, is to have kept your soul alive. — Robert Louis Stevenson
berserker: The curious paradox is that when I accept myself just as I am, then I can change. — Carl Rogers
We cannot solve our problems with the same thinking we used when we created them. — Albert Einstein
When your opponent dominates the center, your normal patterns break open. That is an opportunity. A single focused capacity can cut through the imbalance.
finished: It is not the mountain we conquer, but ourselves. — Edmund Hillary
What you are is God's gift to you. What you become is your gift to God. — Hans Urs von Balthasar
The person who keeps moving toward the center shapes who they become. The person who waits lets imbalance decide for them.
